page_content: SURV600 Fundamentals of Survey and Data Science (3 Credits) Introduces the student to a set of principles of survey and data science that are the basis of standard practices in these ﬁelds. The course exposes the student to key terminology and concepts of collecting and analyzing data from surveys and other data sources to gain insights and to test hypotheses about the nature of human and social behavior and interaction. It will also present a framework that will allow the student to evaluate the influence of different error sources on the quality of data. Prerequisite: Any University of Maryland approved college-level statistics course. Restriction: Permission of BSOS-Joint Program in Survey Methodology department; and must have graduate student status. Credit Only Granted for: SURV699M, SURV400 or SURV600.
